mechanism_narrative: A positive trailing change in FEDFUNDS raises the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and tightens financing conditions, so higher recent policy-rate momentum predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS latest available value was 3.64 as of one month before the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month FEDFUNDS changes are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward observations, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC is above -0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation momentum crosses upward, investors price a higher probability of delayed monetary easing, which raises real-rate expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth crosses above its trailing 12-month growth rate
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL latest available value was 326.588 as of one month before the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not have lower average 20-day S&P 500 returns than non-triggered observations at p < 0.10, or if the sign of the fitted CPIAUCSL interaction coefficient is positive.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp upward crossing in UNRATE signals deteriorating labor demand and rising recession risk, causing expected earnings revisions to fall and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses above its 6-month moving average by more than 0.3 percentage points
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE latest available value was 4.3 as of one month before the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if triggered instances produce positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases and PAYEMS growth does not weaken in the subsequent two monthly releases.

mechanism_narrative: Joint strength in RSAFS and PAYEMS indicates household income growth is converting into realized spending, supporting corporate revenues and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth is positive and PAYEMS 3-month growth is positive
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS latest available value was 734967.0 as of two months before the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if the joint positive RSAFS and PAYEMS trigger has a 60-day mean forward return below the unconditional mean in two consecutive 5-year walk-forward windows.

mechanism_narrative: A downward break in HOUST signals weakening interest-rate-sensitive demand and lower future construction activity, reducing cyclical earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses below its trailing 12-month moving average by more than 10%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST latest available value was 1246.0 as of four months before the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST downside breaks are followed by above-median 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered observations, including after controlling for FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: A positive trend in INDPRO reflects rising production volumes and firmer operating leverage, so stronger industrial output momentum predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO latest available value was 102.325 as of two months before the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if the INDPRO 6-month momentum signal has a walk-forward Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window or positive momentum periods underperform negative momentum periods.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling while UNRATE is rising, rate cuts are being interpreted as a response to recession rather than a growth stimulus, so earnings-risk repricing produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS 3-month change is negative and UNRATE 3-month change is positive
failure_condition: Reject if instances with falling FEDFUNDS and rising UNRATE have average 20-day S&P 500 returns that are not below the unconditional average, or if the interaction term is nonnegative in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A downside CPIAUCSL momentum crossing with FEDFUNDS still elevated signals disinflation without immediate policy exhaustion, increasing expected room for future easing and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth crosses below its trailing 12-month growth rate while FEDFUNDS is above 3%
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS latest available value was 3.64 as of one month before the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if triggered disinflation observations have a 5-day hit rate below 50% or if their average 5-day forward return is not higher than the unconditional average at p < 0.10.
